observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended that the:
 Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant
submit to the Audit Team the Report on Local Road Networks, Road Map,
updated LRNPCs and LRNLCs, for verification; and
 In case of damaged roads, road rehabilitation/ improvement and re-
blocking, proper documentation be prepared by the Municipal Engineer
and furnish the same to the General Services Office and Accounting Office
for the proper reconciliation with LRNPCs and LRNLCs and necessary
derecognition in the books of accounts of the old/replaced and previously
recorded Road Networks or recognition of impairment losses;
 Municipal Accountant –
 Submit the lapsing schedule for CYs 2020 to 2023 for examination, and
ensure its submission in the ensuing years, to support the provision of
depreciation on Road Networks; and
 Henceforth, make sure that the required data on Road Networks System
be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements pursuant to
Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008, to come up with more
informative Financial Statements

Management comments/actions:
1.12 The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant committed to submit the said
reports.

2.12 We recommended that Management:
 Instruct the Inventory Committee of the MLGU to:
 Conduct yearly physical count and inventory of PPE. See to it the
prescribed template - Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) be adopted and contain the required
important details on PPE. For the unserviceable /damaged property and
equipment, use the template – Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP);
 Ensure that individual property number be provided and Property
Sticker be duly accomplished and pasted on the prominent but secure
portion of the property for easy identification; and
 Furnish a copy of the RPCPPE to the General Services Offices and
Accounting Office for reconciliation with their PPE records. Submit
also a copy to the Audit Team for verification not later than January 31
of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS.
 To establish effective management and control over PPE:
 Instruct the General Services Officer to keep Property Card for each
PPE item; and maintain ARE /PAR for every equipment/property
issued to Municipal officials and employees and ensure that the same
be renewed every after three years pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM,
Volume 1;
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated
PPELC for each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS
Manual for LGUs to provide ready information on the accounts and to
support balances in the financial statements; and
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lapsing schedule to
the audit team for verification.

Management comments/actions:
2.13 For the Property Sticker, the Management replied that only the newly purchased
equipment, furniture and fixtures have no property sticker/tag yet.

2.14 The Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for
the (a) Information and Communication Technology Equipment, (b)Disaster
Response and Rescue Equipment, (c) Furniture and Fixtures and (d) Machinery was
submitted on July 03, 2024.

2.15 They further committed to comply with the other audit recommendations.
Auditor’s rejoinder:
2.16 It was noted that several items in the submitted RPCPPE do not have values, also
some damaged items were included. The report is subject for further verification.

additional deduction (receivable from employee) be accordingly
reflected in the payroll; and
25.17.4 always compute, withhold and deduct taxes accordingly from the
salaries of employees, pursuant to Section 24 (2) of NIRC as
amended by R. A. No. 10963 and other pertinent provisions
thereof. Compute year-end adjustments for all employees to
ensure that the tax withheld would be equivalent to tax due, and
that additional deduction or refund be effected, as necessary.

26.6 We have recommended that Management -
26.6.1 submit justification for the above delayed remittances; and
26.6.2 always pay/remit mandatory contributions and loan amortizations
within the scheduled due date prescribed under HDMF Circular
No. 275 to avoid possible inconvenience on the part of municipal
employees.

27.3 We have recommended that Management enforce settlements of the
above suspensions and disallowances.

We have strongly recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Employees and Officials for purposes not authorized under COA Circular No. 97-002. In no case shall cash advance be granted except in compliance with the aforementioned circumstances. Henceforth, the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances should be made in accordance with the law.

We have recommended that management strictly observe the rules and regulations prescribed in Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.

We have recommended that management ensure that all expenditures for repairs and maintenance of equipment are fully supported with relevant documentations.  The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall ensure the completeness of attachments before any payment for repairs and maintenance of equipment are made so as to assure the validity and legality of the transaction.

That the Municipal Budget Officer be instructed to strictly comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations shall be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and Balances; and 
The Municipal Accountant be instructed not to process claims without the required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses will be charged.

All procurement be meticulously planned and that the same shall be within the approved budget of the LGU.  The reimbursement method of procurement shall be sparingly used and resort to such method shall only be in cases of extreme emergency and some other unforeseen and unavoidable situations; and 

Ensure proper and complete documentation of all their future procurements in accordance with the requirements of existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that management especially the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) explain the reasons for not abiding with the rules on procurement. Purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value items should be done through competitive bidding as mandated by RA 9184. In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government of the Philippines is obtained.

-Thorough   evaluation of bids should be conducted by the Bids and Awards Committee; a bidder should be disqualified if he fails to submit the required financial and technical documents.

-Invitation to bid/ Request for Expression of Interest should be published in the PhilGEPs website and procurement costing P 5 million and above should be advertised in a newspaper in a nationwide circulation.

-The BAC members should attend trainings on RA 9184 to familiarize themselves of the provisions of the law.

-Observers should be invited in all stages of procurement

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and payments for contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We further recommend that purchases through cash or reimbursements should be minimized if not totally eliminated.

We reiterated our recommendation that management:
Exert extra effort for the submission of the indispensable documents under COA Circular No. 2016-005 so as to fully support the pending request for write-off already filed in the COA-Regional Office.
Also, that management instruct the accounting unit to maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivable and liability accounts in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS.
We recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the bases in the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor verify and or authenticate the records provided by the claimants with regard to the questioned lands. Likewise, Management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT in the name of LGU.
We recommended that the accountant verify the nature of the prepayments after which, make the adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate should trace back transaction in the previous years in order to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjusting journal entries to reconcile the balances. The Municipal Accountant – Designate should take extra care in recording and posting of transactions involving reciprocal accounts.

We have recommended that management immediately submit the foregoing disbursement vouchers to COA for evaluation and review.  Henceforth, submit reports and DVs within the prescribed deadlines, else, appropriate sanctions shall be imposed.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Employees and Officials for purposes not authorized under COA Circular No. 97-002.  In no case shall cash advance be granted except in compliance with the aforementioned circumstances. Henceforth, the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances be made in accordance with the law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the SB authorization or resolution, and contract of services/job orders as required under the aforementioned regulations.  Henceforth, evaluate and see to it that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents before payment is effected to establish the validity of the transactions as well as the correctness of the accounts used.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management strictly observe the rules and regulations prescribed in Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.  Henceforth, ensure that all necessary documentations are attached to disbursement vouchers before payments for fuel and gasoline consumptions are made.
We reiterated our recommendation for management to ensure that all expenditures for repairs and maintenance of equipment are fully supported with relevant documentations.  Also, for the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make certain the completeness of attachments before any payment for repairs and maintenance of equipment are made so as to assure the validity and legality of the transaction.

Ensure that all procurement be meticulously planned and that the same is within the approved budget of the LGU.  Also, make sure that the resort to reimbursement method of procurement be sparingly used and that the same may be resorted to only in cases of extreme emergency and some other unforeseen and unavoidable situations; and
Ensure proper and complete documentation of all their future procurements in accordance with the requirements of existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the submission of daily time records, leave forms and other pertinent documents to support the payment of salaries to employees.  Likewise, refrain from releasing/paying salaries to persons other than the employees without proper authorization. Stop payments of salary without the signature of the concerned employee or without proper authorization and payments of net take home pay below Four Thousand Pesos.

We recommended that management prepare APP which will serve as a guide in the procurement activities.  Require every Unit to prepare their PPMP for consolidation to APP.  The BAC observe the date of implementation/procurement of the items listed in the APP and see to it that the appropriate mode of procurement are strictly followed.

That the Municipal Budget Officer comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Ensure that the Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and Balances; and
The Municipal Accountant not to process claims without the required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses will be charged.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all their supporting documents after payment and indicate the number of the check issued and the date of actual payment to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims, thereby safeguarding government resources against loss or wastage.

We recommended that copy of the contract documents and Purchase Orders be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance in the standard form as prescribed by GPPB and as required under COA Circular 2009-001 for auditorial and technical review.

We recommended that all Memoranda of Agreement entered into by the municipality or Notice of Authority to Debit Account be submitted to the audit team for verification if the specific purpose to which the fund was established has been met.

We recommended that management, especially the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), explain the reasons for not following the rules on procurement. Henceforth, ensure that purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value items be done through competitive bidding as mandated by RA 9184.  In all instances, ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government of the Philippines is obtained.
We recommended that management ensure full implementation of its Solid Waste Management Plan in order to fully realize its objective.
Submit the required SEF Budget approved by the majority of Local School Boards for proper evaluation;

prepare and submit the Report on SEF Utilization in compliance with the provisions of Item 6.0 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and
Ensure the full implementation of the activities in the SEF budget to maximize the benefit that the constituents could have received.

We recommended that an Investment Plan and Utilization Report for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund be prepared following the prescribed format presented in the Annexes of COA Circular 2012-001.  Also, comply with the guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed under Republic Act 10121.

That the municipality utilize the unexpended balance for the intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and to accordingly re-appropriate the funds of PPAs that have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertakings; and
Follow programmed timelines for period of implementation in the AIP to ensure the full implementation of the development projects.  Prepare project implementation schedule in order to effectively implement and monitor all the projects of the municipality.

We recommended that management instruct the in-charge or the Planning and Development Office to amend or update the Annual Investment Plan in case of realignment of appropriations through SB Resolutions.  Submit the corresponding realignment/supplemental appropriations ordinance for evaluation.

Ensure the full implementation of the activities in the GPB to maximize the benefit that the constituents could have received; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of JMC 2013-01 on the localization of the Magna Carta for Women (MCW).  Refer to the full text of the JMC for details and guidance on the implementation of the MCW which can be downloaded from the PCW website (http://www.pcw.gov.ph).

We have recommended that management instructs the municipal treasurer to remit taxes on or before due date to avoid incurrence of penalty.  Submit justification for the late payment otherwise the said amount will be disallowed in audit.  Henceforth, ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in all the operations of the agency to safeguard its resources against losses and/or wastages.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and payments for contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We further recommend that purchases through cash or reimbursements be minimized, if not totally eliminated.

That the Municipal Accountant exert additional effort to establish and determine the nature of the accounts.  Consider measures which include sending demand letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations.  If the receivables are finally found to be uncollectible and unrecoverable, a request for write-off may be considered pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations.

Also, that management instruct the accounting unit to maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivable and liability accounts in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the bases in the preparation of the financial statements.
